Krugman](blob:http://localhost/b9a31d3949b1882a09ed2f8508d538f3)](https://www.masterclass.com/classes/crypto-and", "relevance_score": 0.85}, {"title": "TotalAddressableMarket- What Is TAM & How to Calculate It | Toptal", "url": "https://www.toptal.com/management-consultants/market-sizing/total-addressable-market-example", "snippet": "[Jina Parallel Content] Top down and bottom up methods usually look at existing paradigms and assume that a new offering will fit into them. Yet, how could one assess the opportunity for, say, flying cars, when flying cars don\u2019t yet exist? For products or services that can evolve a market into a new state and/or provide value-add to different groups of consumers, the value theory may be the best option. With value theory, one must assess how much a customer would be willing to pay for an improvement/evolution of a product. Such an exercise would have been useful when music streaming subscription services, like Spotify, started to roll out. At that time, consumers would pay a fixed price to buy music, yet the value-add of not owning a song but having access to a large library of them to effectively \u201cborrow\u201d was such that the common pricing we see now of $9.99 per month was deemed value-additive. Attaching that pricing to the number of music listeners would have shown the TAM more appropriately than simply looking at how much was spent at the time on purchased downloads. ### What WeWork\u2019s TAM Would Be To start, I will define what WeWork is in the broadest sense, within the SAM and SOM sections I will go deeper into its true positioning and service offering. For that, WeWork is in the market of providing work space to service sector workers, on a subscription basis: space as a service (SpaaS), perhaps? Between the three methodologies, I chose to use a top down method, starting with employment for a select group of countries. The bottom up methodology would be far too sensitive to apply to different geographies. The value theory method could be appropriate for such a transcendent company, but the subjective nature of it could turn what is intended to be a tutorial into an anthropological study into the monetary value of networking. I will now explain the top down rationale that I will use, step by step: 1.   I defined my geographic universe as the [35 members of the OECD](http://www.oecd.org/about/membersandpartners/list-oecd-member-countries.htm) and five other countries where WeWork currently has operations (Argentina, Brazil, China, Colombia and India). 2.   Using a combination of OECD and UN data, I worked out the percentage of the labor force working in services. This was then trimmed to desk jobs using US [BLS](https://www.bls.gov/) data for the ratio of relevant subset desk jobs to the entire service sector. 3.   Using more OECD data, I stratified the workforce by the size of the company that the employees work for, assuming that smaller companies/entrepreneurs would use shared desks at WeWork, but companies of over 10 employees would want more expensive private offices. 4.   Using quoted prices from a random WeWork office in each US city that it [operates in](https://www.wework.com/locations) (this was 22 cities at the time), I came up with a composite price for a hot/dedicated desk and private office seat of $378 and $610 respectively. I assumed that this is a fair", "relevance_score": 0.85}, {"title": "What IsTotalAvailableMarket(TAM)? A 2025 Guide", "url": "https://eathealthy365.com/total-available-market-tam-explained-for-founders/", "snippet": "[Jina Parallel Content] *   [Where can I find reliable data for my TAM calculation?](https://eathealthy365.com/total-available-market-tam-explained-for-founders/#where-can-i-find-reliable-data-for-my-tam-calculation) *   [What are the most common mistakes when calculating TAM?](https://eathealthy365.com/total-available-market-tam-explained-for-founders/#what-are-the-most-common-mistakes-when-calculating-tam) *   [How often should I recalculate my company\u2019s TAM?](https://eathealthy365.com/total-available-market-tam-explained-for-founders/#how-often-should-i-recalculate-my-companys-tam) *   [Frequently Asked Questions](https://eathealthy365.com/total-available-market-tam-explained-for-founders/#frequently-asked-questions) *   [What is meant by total available market (TAM)?](https://eathealthy365.com/total-available-market-tam-explained-for-founders/#what-is-meant-by-total-available-market-tam) *   [What is the difference between TAM, SAM, and SOM?](https://eathealthy365.com/total-available-market-tam-explained-for-founders/#what-is-the-difference-between-tam-sam-and-som) *   [Why is TAM important for investors?](https://eathealthy365.com/total-available-market-tam-explained-for-founders/#why-is-tam-important-for-investors) *   [What are the two main ways to calculate TAM?](https://eathealthy365.com/total-available-market-tam-explained-for-founders/#what-are-the-two-main-ways-to-calculate-tam) *   [Conclusion](https://eathealthy365.com/total-available-market-tam-explained-for-founders/#conclusion) ## Understanding the Core Concepts: TAM, SAM, and SOM Before we dive deeper into TAM, it\u2019s crucial to understand it in context. TAM rarely exists in a vacuum. It\u2019s the first and largest of three key market-sizing metrics: TAM, SAM, and SOM. Seeing them together paints a much more realistic picture of your true business opportunity. ### What is the difference between TAM, SAM, and SOM? Imagine you\u2019re opening a new, high-end vegan pizza restaurant. The relationship between TAM, SAM, and SOM is like a set of Russian nesting dolls, each fitting inside the other, moving from the broadest market to your specific, reachable slice. *   **Total Available Market (TAM):** This is the total global demand for your type of product. For our example, this could be the total annual revenue of the entire restaurant market in your country. It\u2019s everyone who eats out, regardless of what they eat or where they live. *   **Serviceable Available Market (SAM):** This is the segment of the TAM that you can actually serve with your current business model. For your vegan pizza place, the SAM would be the total revenue from all restaurants within a reasonable delivery or driving distance that cater to customers looking for pizza or vegan options. You\u2019re filtering out steak-lovers and people who live 100 miles away. *   **Serviceable Obtainable Market (SOM):** Also called the Share of Market, this is the realistic portion of the SAM that you can capture in the short term, considering your competition, market", "relevance_score": 0.85}]
